sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Diabetes management and weight loss support.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a history of diabetes, currently managed with medication. He also reports experiencing some acne and occasional foot swelling. He has been taking clindamycin topical and benzoyl peroxide for acne, and is interested in exploring more affordable options. He mentions a coworker with a thyroid issue who suggested chromium for weight loss, and a cousin suggested amino acids. The patient's birth control was discontinued in December, and he's concerned about potential weight gain related to that. He also reports a vitamin D deficiency and has been taking unithroid with a discount card. He completed the COVID vaccine and has no known allergies.
Findings
The patient's total cholesterol was 222 mg/dL with a good cholesterol of 44 mg/dL and a non-HDL cholesterol of 178 mg/dL. His ALT was 128 U/L. Salivary cortisol results were generally normal, though one was abnormal. The dexamethasone suppression test was negative. Liver enzymes have improved since discontinuation of birth control. The patient's BMI is greater than 30, potentially qualifying him for the weight loss study. Peroxide may bleach sheets. The patient's vitamin D level is low.
Assessment

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The patient has type 2 diabetes and is experiencing acne, foot swelling, and a vitamin D deficiency. He is also considering options for weight loss.
Plan
I will order labs for next time and continue monitoring the patient's vitamin D levels. I will also order a prescription for phexxi as a contraceptive option. I will also order a prescription for unithroid to help with the thyroid issue. I will also recommend a topical antibiotic and benzoyl peroxide for acne. I will schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess progress and adjust treatment as needed.
